get.summary:
ListObjects returns objects from a bucket, optionally filtered by
key prefix and paginated.

parameters.name: keyPrefix
parameters.description:
Optional key prefix. Only objects whose key starts with this prefix
are returned.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of objects to return. The server may cap the value.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous ListObjectsResponse.

get.summary:
GetObject reads an Object's metadata. It does not return bytes; use
GetObjectDownloadURL to read bytes.

GetObjectDownloadURL returns a presigned URL the client can GET to
fetch the bytes of a READY object directly.

post.summary:
FinalizeObjectUpload transitions an Object from PENDING to READY
after the client has successfully PUT the bytes to the presigned
URL.

InitiateObjectUpload reserves an Object in PENDING state and returns
a presigned URL the client should PUT bytes to directly.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
InitiateObjectUploadRequest is the request message for
InitiateObjectUpload.

get.summary:
ListRecords returns records from a collection, optionally filtered
by attributes, paginated, and ordered.

parameters.name: attributeFilters
parameters.description:
Optional exact-match filters over indexed attributes. Multiple
filters are combined with logical AND. Only exact-match is
supported in v1; range queries and JOINs are out of scope.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return. The server may cap the value.

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous ListRecordsResponse.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Implementation-defined order specification. Conventional format is
"<field> <asc|desc>", for example "created_at desc". Empty string
means implementation-default order.

get.summary: GetRecord reads a single record by collection and key.

delete.summary:
DeleteRecord removes a record. Uses optimistic concurrency when
if_version is set.

post.summary:
PutRecord writes a record to a collection. Uses optimistic
concurrency when if_version is set.

StorageFinalizeObjectUploadBody.description:
FinalizeObjectUploadRequest is the request message for
FinalizeObjectUpload.

attributes.description: Indexed attributes for filtering and ordering.

contentType.description: MIME-ish hint describing how to decode data.

ifVersion.description:
Optional optimistic-concurrency check. When set, the RPC fails with
FAILED_PRECONDITION if the current version of the record does not
match this value. Use 0 to require that the record does not yet
exist.

idempotencyKey.description:
Optional idempotency key. Replay-safe mutations — the same key from
the same caller within the implementation-defined window returns
the original response.

StoragePutRecordBody.description: PutRecordRequest is the request message for PutRecord.

v1DeleteObjectResponse.description:
DeleteObjectResponse is the response message for DeleteObject. It is
intentionally empty.

v1DeleteRecordResponse.description:
DeleteRecordResponse is the response message for DeleteRecord. It is
intentionally empty.

object.description: The finalized object in READY state.

v1FinalizeObjectUploadResponse.description:
FinalizeObjectUploadResponse is the response message for
FinalizeObjectUpload.

downloadUrl.description: Presigned URL the client can GET to fetch the object bytes directly.

expiresAt.description: When the presigned URL becomes invalid.

v1GetObjectDownloadURLResponse.description:
GetObjectDownloadURLResponse is the response message for
GetObjectDownloadURL.

object.description:
The requested object's metadata. Bytes are not returned; use
GetObjectDownloadURL to fetch bytes.

v1GetObjectResponse.description: GetObjectResponse is the response message for GetObject.

v1GetRecordResponse.description: GetRecordResponse is the response message for GetRecord.

contentType.description: MIME type the client will upload.

sizeBytes.description:
Expected size of the object in bytes. The server may reject uploads
that exceed this value at finalization time.

metadata.description: User-defined metadata to be preserved alongside the object.

v1InitiateObjectUploadRequest.description:
InitiateObjectUploadRequest is the request message for
InitiateObjectUpload.

object.description: The registered object in PENDING state.

uploadUrl.description: Presigned URL the client must PUT the bytes to directly.

uploadHeaders.description:
Headers the client must include on the PUT request, typically
including Content-Type and any signature-related headers required
by the backend.

expiresAt.description: When the presigned URL and any required headers become invalid.

v1InitiateObjectUploadResponse.description:
InitiateObjectUploadResponse is the response message for
InitiateObjectUpload.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further results.

v1ListObjectsResponse.description: ListObjectsResponse is the response message for ListObjects.

nextPageToken.description: Opaque pagination token. Empty when there are no further results.

v1ListRecordsResponse.description: ListRecordsResponse is the response message for ListRecords.

bucket.description:
Logical grouping of objects, pre-created out of band by operators.
Implementations map buckets to S3 buckets, GCS buckets, MinIO
buckets, filesystem directories, or any other backend-specific
concept.

key.description: Path within the bucket. May contain slashes for hierarchical keys.

contentType.description: MIME type of the object content.

sizeBytes.description:
Size of the object in bytes, as reported by the backend after
finalization.

etag.description:
Implementation-defined content identity. Typically the S3/GCS ETag
or a content hash. Opaque to the spec.

metadata.description:
User-defined metadata preserved by the implementation alongside the
object.

uploadState.description: Upload state. Only READY objects are durable.

createdAt.description:
Time the object was first created (typically at
InitiateObjectUpload).

updatedAt.description: Time the object was most recently updated or finalized.

v1Object.description:
Object is a blob of bytes identified by (bucket, key) and served via
presigned URLs. Objects are the unstructured half of Storage.

record.description:
The written record, including the server-assigned version and
timestamps.

v1PutRecordResponse.description: PutRecordResponse is the response message for PutRecord.

collection.description:
Opaque collection name (for example "runs", "events", "skills").
Collections are not declared in the spec; domain services and
implementations name their own.

key.description:
Primary key within the collection. Opaque to Storage; meaningful to
the caller.

attributes.description:
Indexed key/value attributes used by ListRecords for filtering and
ordering. Storage guarantees attributes are queryable; the data
blob is not.

data.description: Opaque payload. Format is caller-defined and hinted by content_type.

contentType.description:
MIME-ish hint describing how to decode data (for example
"application/x-protobuf", "application/json", "text/plain"). Storage
does not validate the payload shape.

version.description:
Monotonic version number assigned by Storage on every write. Used
with PutRecord and DeleteRecord if_version fields for optimistic
concurrency.

createdAt.description: Time the record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Time the record was most recently updated.

v1Record.description:
Record is a single structured entry in a named collection. Records are
the structured-KV half of Storage. Every piece of control-plane state
that is not a blob — runs, events, user configs, skills, agents,
connectors, billing events, schedules, artifact metadata — is a Record.

The (collection, key) pair is the primary identity. Attributes are the
indexed, filterable metadata used by ListRecords. Data is an opaque
payload whose format is decided by the caller (typically serialized
protobuf or JSON, hinted by content_type). The version field enables
optimistic concurrency on mutations.

v1UploadState.description:
UploadState is the lifecycle state of an Object during and after
upload.

 - UPLOAD_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: The upload state is unknown. This is the proto3 default and should
not be returned by conforming implementations.
 - UPLOAD_STATE_PENDING: The object has been registered via InitiateObjectUpload but no bytes
have been confirmed yet. Implementations should garbage-collect
PENDING objects that are not finalized within an implementation-
defined window.
 - UPLOAD_STATE_READY: The object upload has been finalized and the bytes are durable.
GetObjectDownloadURL will return a presigned URL.
 - UPLOAD_STATE_FAILED: The object upload failed and the bytes are not durable. Implementations
should garbage-collect FAILED objects eventually.
